file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Lint
        run: nr lint

  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: lts/*

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Typecheck
        run: nr typecheck

  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    strategy:
      matrix:
        node: [24.x, 22.x]
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        include:
          - node: 20.x
            os: ubuntu-latest
      fail-fast: false

    permissions:
      id-token: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v3

      - name: Set node ${{ matrix.node }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}

      - name: Setup
        run: npm i -g @antfu/ni

      - name: Install
        run: nci

      - name: Build
        run: nr build

      - name: Test
        run: nr test --coverage

      - name: Upload coverage reports to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          use_oidc: true

The `nci` command from @antfu/ni is a smart install command that uses the detected package manager (pnpm in this case).

Now let me create the Dockerfile and run.sh for Phase 2.

Key points:
- Use ubuntu:22.04 (since macos-latest isn't available in Docker)
- Install Node.js 24.x
- Install pnpm 10.31.0 (as specified in packageManager)
- Set up the environment
- Run the tests

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV FORCE_COLOR=3

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js 24.x
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_24.x | bash - && \
    apt-get install -y nodejs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install pnpm
RUN npm install -g pnpm@10.31.0

# Install @antfu/ni
RUN npm install -g @antfu/ni

WORKDIR /app

COPY repo/ /app/

# Install dependencies
RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

# Build
RUN pnpm -r run build

COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

- Base image: `ubuntu:22.04`
- Installs Node.js 24.x from NodeSource
- Installs pnpm 10.31.0 (as specified in package.json)
- Installs @antfu/ni for smart package manager commands
- Copies the repository code
- Runs `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- Runs `pnpm -r run build` to build all packages

### run.sh
- Executes `pnpm vitest --coverage` to run tests with code coverage
- Prints `FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS` upon completion

### Test Results
The container successfully:
- Installed all dependencies
- Built all packages in the workspace
- Ran all unit tests with coverage reporting
- Generated coverage statistics across the codebase
